waterfall.contract:
DATA MODEL: start_value + sum(signed_deltas) = end_value
HOW TO READ:
  - Each bar represents one delta in the running total
  - Bar goes UP from running total -> positive delta
  - Bar goes DOWN from running total -> negative delta
  - If legend present: use it to confirm sign interpretation
  - If no legend: bar direction relative to running total IS the sign
  - Start and end bars are full-height solid bars anchored at zero
  - Delta bars float above or hang below the running total line
  - Unit may be currency (e.g. S$m) OR percentage points (ppt) — infer from axis label
CONSTRAINT: sum all deltas, verify start + sum = end
  If arithmetic fails, your signs are wrong — flip bars until it balances
EXTRACT AS: one DataPoint per bar
  series = bar label as printed on the slide
  value  = signed delta (e.g. '+658', '-1,236') or start/end value
  row_type = 'start' | 'bridge' | 'end'
  sign = '+' or '-' for bridge bars only

stacked_bar.contract:
DATA MODEL: for each period, components stack bottom-to-top summing to total
HOW TO READ:
  - Read each segment value from its printed label
  - Bottom segment = first component in legend, top = last
  - Total = sum of all segments for that period (usually printed above bar)
  - Period axis = x-axis labels (quarters, fiscal years, dates)
  - Negative segments: if a segment visually extends below zero baseline, its value is negative
  - Single-component bars (no stacking): treat identically — one DataPoint per period, same rules
  - Supplementary metric rows printed below the bars (e.g. averages, ratios): extract as
    separate series with the same period labels
  - MULTI-YEAR GROUPED CHARTS: if the x-axis groups quarters by year (e.g. 2023 1Q 2Q 3Q 4Q | 2024 1Q 2Q 3Q 4Q), the period MUST include the year: '2023 1Q', '2024 2Q' etc. Never use bare '1Q' when multiple years are present — it is ambiguous.
CONSTRAINT: sum of components per period = printed total above bar
  Rounding tolerance: accept +/-1 unit discrepancy
EXTRACT AS: one DataPoint per (component, period)
  series = component name (from legend)
  period = x-axis label as printed — include year when grouped multi-year chart
  value  = segment value as printed
  extra_fields: { 'bar_total': printed total above bar if present }

stacked_bar_with_overlay.contract:
DATA MODEL: stacked bars (same summation model as stacked_bar) PLUS one or more trend lines on the same or secondary axis
WHY DISTINCT: the overlay line has NO summation relationship to the bars and often uses a different scale — it must be extracted as a separate series, not as a bar component
HOW TO READ:
  - Extract all stacked bar segments exactly per the stacked_bar contract
  - Then extract each overlay line as a SEPARATE series
  - Overlay lines are typically ratios or rates: %, bps, ppt — read the axis label or line label to confirm unit
  - Each overlay point has a printed value — read it directly, do not interpolate
  - The overlay series and bar series share the same x-axis periods
CONSTRAINT:
  - Bars: sum of components = bar total (per stacked_bar rules)
  - Overlay lines: no summation constraint
EXTRACT AS:
  - All stacked bar DataPoints (as per stacked_bar contract)
  - One DataPoint per (overlay_series_name, period)
    series = overlay line label as printed
    period = x-axis label
    value  = printed value on the overlay line
    extra_fields: { 'series_type': 'overlay_line', 'unit': unit read from slide }

trend_line.contract:
DATA MODEL: for each series, one value per time period
HOW TO READ:
  - Each line = one series, identified by label at line end or in legend
  - Each point value is printed above or below the point
  - X-axis = periods, Y-axis = values
  - Multiple lines = multiple series, extract all
  - Use this contract when trend lines appear WITHOUT accompanying bar charts
  - When a trend line overlays bars, use stacked_bar_with_overlay instead
CONSTRAINT: none
EXTRACT AS: one DataPoint per (series, period)

pie.contract:
DATA MODEL: segments summing to total (100% or absolute)
HOW TO READ:
  - Each segment has label and value
  - If % shown: should sum to ~100%
  - If absolute: should sum to printed total
  - Centre label if present = grand total
CONSTRAINT: sum of segments ≈ 100% or centre total
EXTRACT AS: one DataPoint per segment

donut_dual_ring.contract:
DATA MODEL: two concentric donut rings representing the same composition breakdown for two different time periods
WHY DISTINCT: inner and outer rings encode different periods spatially — the period each ring represents varies by slide and must be read from the slide itself
HOW TO READ:
  - READ THE CENTRE LABELS FIRST — each ring has a period label (e.g. 'FY24', 'FY25', '2024', '1H25') printed at or near the centre
  - The label closest to the inner ring = the inner ring's period; the label closest to the outer ring = the outer ring's period
  - Do NOT assume inner = earlier or outer = later — always read the actual labels
  - Segment labels float outside the outer ring — each segment has two values (one per ring); match by radial position
  - The value visually closer to the centre belongs to the inner ring's period
  - The value visually closer to the outside belongs to the outer ring's period
  - Each ring's segments should sum to ~100%
  - If two separate donut charts appear side by side (not concentric), treat each as an independent pie
CONSTRAINT: each ring sums to ~100%; rounding tolerance +/-2ppt
EXTRACT AS: one DataPoint per (segment_label, period)
  series = segment name (e.g. 'Global Wholesale Banking')
  period = the period label read from the slide (e.g. 'FY25') — NOT 'inner' or 'outer'
  value  = percentage as printed

kpi_grid.contract:
DATA MODEL: individual metrics, no summation relationship
HOW TO READ:
  - Each KPI has label, current value, and often change vs prior period
  - Change may be absolute, ppt, %, or bps — extract all that are printed
  - Change direction (+/-) is critical — preserve sign
  - Period labels (FY25, 4Q25) identify which column the KPI belongs to
  - If KPIs are grouped into labelled panels (e.g. 'Greater China', 'United States'),
    the panel header is the segment — include it in the label or extra_fields
CONSTRAINT: none
EXTRACT AS: one DataPoint per KPI per period
  extra_fields: { 'change_value': ..., 'change_type': 'YoY' | 'QoQ', 'change_unit': '%' | 'ppt' | 'bps', 'segment': panel header if applicable }

text_table.contract:
DATA MODEL: rows and columns, bold rows = totals, indented = sub-items
HOW TO READ:
  - Column headers define the value dimensions
  - Bold label = total or subtotal row
  - Indented label = sub-item of the bold row above it
  - Grey/shaded cell = not applicable, value = empty string
  - Parentheses = negative value, keep verbatim
  - Dash or em-dash in a cell = zero change or flat; preserve as string
  - 'nm' = not meaningful, 'na' = not applicable — preserve as strings
  - YoY/QoQ columns contain % changes — preserve sign and % symbol
CONSTRAINT: sub-items should sum to their parent total
EXTRACT AS: one DataPoint per (row, column)
  extra_fields: { 'row_type': 'total' | 'subtotal' | 'line_item', 'indent_level': 0 | 1 | 2 }

npa_movement_table.contract:
DATA MODEL: NPA roll-forward — opening balance + signed inflows/outflows = closing balance, across multiple period columns
WHY DISTINCT FROM text_table: has a mandatory arithmetic constraint (roll-forward must balance) and a specific sign convention for outflows
HOW TO READ:
  - 'At start of period' bold row = opening NPA balance for each period column
  - Flow items:
      New NPAs = positive (increases NPA stock)
      Recoveries/upgrades = negative (shown in parentheses on slide)
      Write-offs = negative (shown in parentheses on slide)
  - Section subtotal = net movement for that section
  - FX/translation adjustment = can be positive or negative, read sign from slide
  - 'At end of period' bold row = closing NPA balance
  - NPL Ratio row = closing NPA / total loans (%); not part of the roll-forward arithmetic
CONSTRAINT: At start + sum(all signed flow items) = At end
  Verify for EVERY period column
  Parentheses = negative — failure to flip signs breaks the check
EXTRACT AS: one DataPoint per (row_label, period_column)
  series = row label as printed
  period = column header as printed
  value  = verbatim as printed including parentheses
  extra_fields: { 'row_type': 'opening' | 'inflow' | 'outflow' | 'subtotal' | 'closing' | 'ratio', 'section': section header }
